title: messages: file name validation does not reject control characters (non-null)
description: deserialize_file_name rejects null bytes, path separators, and . / .. But other control characters (\x01-\x1f, \x7f) are accepted as valid file names. On Windows these characters are illegal in file names; on macOS they are disallowed by the VFS in most cases. A hostile peer sending a file name like 'evil\x01.txt' would pass validation here, then either cause an OS-level error at write time (confusing to users) or create an unreadable file. Add: if s.chars().any(|c| c.is_control()) { return Err(...) }. The null byte check is already there; this extends it to all control characters.
